rules: Every substantial active task has exactly one Context checkpoint section with one fenced YAML block.
All required_fields exist at the top level.
next_action appears exactly once at the top level and is a non-empty concrete scalar rather than a placeholder.
Validation result values come from allowed_validation_results.
A normalized exact fact cannot appear in more than one evidence-state list.

repository_specific_scope: Oteryn Platform context routes and architecture/document routing.
Delivery, merge, security, Laravel, database, and cross-repository policies outside the shared checkpoint/handoff structure.
upgrade_required_when: Canary changes the shared checkpoint version, required checkpoint fields, status values, validation result values, evidence-state semantics, next_action cardinality, or evidence-state conflict rule.
Oteryn Platform intentionally changes any shared checkpoint/handoff rule incompatibly with the declared reference version.
upgrade_process: Inspect the current Canary checkpoint/handoff contract read-only.
Decide whether Oteryn Platform adopts the new shared version or records an intentional divergence.
Update this marker, CONTEXT_HANDOFF.md, TASK_TEMPLATE.md, checkpoint.py, and validator tests together.
Keep repository-specific governance independent; do not mechanically synchronize complete AGENTS.md or routing content.
